config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Kalau prioritasnya selalu masyarakat di atas individu, mudah sekali muncul pembenaran untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau suara yang berbeda.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of statement makes me nervous because history shows how easily “the common good” gets used to excuse overreach, discrimination, or trampling unpopular people. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since laws against harm, fraud, and public danger exist for a reason. So I would say society matters, but it should be limited by strong individual rights rather than placed above them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia sendiri kita tahu betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membungkam kritik atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil. Memang benar masyarakat perlu aturan untuk mencegah bahaya, penipuan, atau kekacauan, tetapi itu justru menunjukkan bahwa hak individu tidak bisa dianggap sekadar penghalang. Buat saya, kepentingan sosial dan hak individu harus diseimbangkan, bukan diperingkatkan secara mutlak. Kalau hak dasar individu dilemahkan, yang sering muncul bukan masyarakat yang lebih sehat, melainkan kekuasaan yang lebih leluasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. In the United States, I have seen how quickly “for the public good” can become a blank check for abuse, whether that means suppressing dissent, targeting minorities, or ignoring due process. Society does need rules to prevent harm and maintain order, but those limits should be narrow and justified, not a general license to override basic rights. To me, a healthy society is one that protects individuals first, because that is what keeps power accountable in the long run.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dalam pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan wewenang. Memang ada situasi tertentu ketika hak individu bisa dibatasi, misalnya untuk mencegah bahaya nyata, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan dalih umum. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu, karena tanpa itu yang kuat akan mudah menginjak yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, the phrase "for the good of society" has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power, so I am wary of giving it automatic precedence. That said, individual rights cannot mean permission to harm others, so reasonable limits for public safety and basic order are necessary. To me, the right balance is protecting individual rights first and then narrowly limiting them only when there is a clear, concrete need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
